title: AAR — Power Play, second segment: box score, presence effect, the toggle, and the stat rack
scope: It records the work done *after* the base rule landed, the open design questions it surfaced, and — importantly — **why the checkbox isn't showing up in the deployed app**.

rationale.item: Records the work done after the base rule landed and explains why the checkbox isn't showing up in the deployed app.
The base rule tracks power-play activity only in game state and none of it survives into the league DB — a full end-to-end stat family is needed in leagues where the rule is on.

delegation_events.decision: Stat-rack scope (defense + short-handed offense + leaderboards/glossary; pitching deferred)

delegation_events.quote: A new `game_power_play_stats` table (+ a season aggregate), rather than bolting always-NULL `pp_*` columns onto `game_batter_stats`/`game_pitcher_stats` for the 99% of leagues that never enable the rule.

delegation_events.decision: Pitching stats deferred as an open question
delegation_events.quote: what (if anything) to measure there is an open question we chose not to guess at in this pass

delegation_events.decision: Per-league vs global toggle posed to the user (then built)
delegation_events.quote: should Power Play become a per-league flag on the new-league builder (recommended, matches "by league"), with the global Engine Settings toggle demoted to a default?

actions.description: Box-score Powerplays footer names the nickel (NF) and his putouts; PO counted per deployment via credit_nickel_putout
Presence effect: banded team-wide defense + pitching multiplicative lift (0.1%–4.4%) on the per-PA stash-and-restore lifecycle
Grouped Power Play + IBB toggles under "Optional rules" on Engine Settings (/engine/settings)

Per-league checkbox: teams.power_play_enabled column (CREATE TABLE + ALTER migration, default 0); checkbox on new_league.html; new_league_post stamps teams; sim.py forces state.power_play_enabled when set (composes as per-league opt-in OR global default)

Stat rack: dedicated game_power_play_stats table; defense PPD/PPO/XBHH/HC/NF-PO; short-handed offense SH-PA/SH-AB/SH-H/SH-AVG; engine snapshot state.power_play_sh_active + short_handed_for_batting(); _extract_power_play_stats; INSERT gated on power_play_on; /leaders Power Play section; glossary "Power Play / Short-handed" section

risk_posture: Gating principle throughout: write/aggregate/display PP stats **only for games/leagues where the rule was on**, so rule-off leagues are untouched.
the rule-off seed-replay identity test still passes (off = byte-for-byte unchanged)

validation_claims.claim: o27/tests/test_power_play.py: 30 cases (box-score formats, presence band scaling, apply/restore, idempotency, inert-when-closed / non-deploying-team, window open/clear)
Power-play + declared-seconds + realism-identity suites green; rule-off seed-replay identity test still passes
Paired-CRN micro (N=300k): top-of-band lift shaves ~0.27% of hits through the fielding channel alone, fading to ~nil at the floor
Per-league flag composition verified: global OFF + league ON ⇒ on; global ON + league None ⇒ on
validation_claims.check_method: Set flag combinations and check state.power_play_enabled

validation_claims.claim: tests/test_power_play_stats.py (5) + engine suite (30) green; rule-off leagues write zero rows and show no section; SH-AB ≤ SH-PA holds with zero violations
validation_claims.check_method: Run tests/test_power_play_stats.py and query the table on a rule-off league

validation_claims.claim: After git fetch origin main: PR #136 is merged, main HEAD is 0be80a2, all five power-play commits on main
validation_claims.check_method: Check git log / PR #136 on GitHub

non_actions.reason: The pitcher backed by the nickel is on the advantaged side, not short-handed; what (if anything) to measure there is an open question we chose not to guess at in this pass.

non_actions.item: (Originally) per-league rule flag — recommended fix deferred pending a decision

residual_risks: SH-H can exceed SH-AB — the engine's Second-Chance mechanic credits multiple hits per AB; the app leads with PA-denominated rates.

Pre-existing unrelated failure: tests/test_template_renders.py::test_season_archive_writer_runs_end_to_end (a wrc_plus category assertion) fails identically with our changes stashed — not caused by this work.
Possible secondary factor: fly.dev deploy lag if the app hadn't rebuilt from the freshly-merged main yet.
open_hooks: Open decisions for the user §6: per-league vs global framing; build the stat rack per §5 (both subsequently built per the Status: BUILT section)
section_headers_original: AAR — Power Play, second segment: box score, presence effect, the toggle, and the stat rack
2. Presence effect — the banded team-wide lift (`fed9749`)
3. The toggle (`0e0c0cc`) — and why it doesn't appear in the app
Why the checkbox seemed missing in the app
5. The stat rack — design (investigated, NOT yet built)
End-to-end extension points (mapped, ready to implement)
unmapped_content: Branch claude/power-play-optional-rule-HmsMs; five commits table (c3d5b8c base rule, c98b4b1 AAR, e50f968 box-score note, fed9749 presence, 0e0c0cc toggle grouping). Correction: an earlier draft claimed the feature was unmerged — wrong, from a stale local origin/main; lesson: always git fetch before asserting merge state. Real reasons checkbox seemed missing: (1) wrong page — it lives on Engine Settings, user's screenshots were the new-league builder; (2) it's global, not per-league, but expectation is per-league (engine_config stores one override blob in sim_meta applying to every league). Terminology note (from review): the side with the nickel is the Power Play side; "short-handed" refers to the offense facing a loaded defense a man down — this drives the §5 stat design. End-to-end extension-points table mapping engine accumulation, carrier, render credit, per-game DB, sim write, season aggregate, leaderboards, glossary with file:line anchors; 4-step build order.
parser_notes: No date in document; 'undated'. Document evolved in place: §5 is titled 'investigated, NOT yet built' but a later 'Status: BUILT' subsection records that the per-league checkbox and stat rack subsequently shipped on the same branch; §6 item 1 marked done.
